campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall spending. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as walking, biking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - making it easier to get around by walking, biking, and transit

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their views on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council voted to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first reading process, allowing them to potentially adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a new ordinance
Council waived the standard first reading requirement to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at the next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to speak for or against the proposed rule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they approved meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorized the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on routine administrative business and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Routine administrative approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving its young people, looking at programs that help keep kids safe during summer and connect them with support services. The council also heard updates on how city code enforcement is working and checked on plans to improve the library system's buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth programs and mentoring (Youth Empowerment Alliance, Summer Safety Initiative)
How well code enforcement is doing its job
Plans to upgrade library buildings and improve customer experience
Youth intervention and counseling services performance

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 13, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 13, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear directly from community members with concerns or ideas, and discuss upcoming city events and activities. Council members broke into smaller groups to dive deeper into specific topics and shared updates on their various committees.
keyTopics: Community members had the opportunity to speak directly to the council about local issues
City is planning special events coming up in Mountain View
Council split into focused subcommittees to tackle specific topics in detail
Committee reports and staff updates on current city projects and decisions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about life in Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents learn about and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who try to influence city decisions to register and publicly report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget discussion and community engagement
Lobbying registration and reporting requirements under Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
New disclosure rules for people attempting to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 13, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a special committee meeting focused on economic development and marketing for the city. The meeting was available both in-person and online, with options for residents to join via Zoom or phone call to watch or submit comments.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meetings
In-person and remote participation options for residents
Written public comment submission process
Zoom and phone call access for public attendance

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning and administrative items for the city. Key discussions included updates to the city's five-year housing and community development plan, consideration of new projects requested by council members, and a potential change to how city council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests from individual council members
Possible change to council vacancy procedures
Approval of meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as more historically significant, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as part of renovation and earthquake safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic retrofitting and rehab
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
